subject: Product Management
title: Listen to Users, Not Just Data
description: A micro-lesson that makes Listen to Users, Not Just Data usable.

hook.text: New Coke won every taste test and failed in the market. Data said yes. Customers said no. The customers were right.

buildup.text: Quantitative data tells you what is happening. Qualitative research — interviews, observation — tells you why.

discovery.text: Snapchat's redesign showed strong engagement metrics initially. But user sentiment was furious — metrics lagged emotions.

twist.text: Teams that rely only on data miss emotional signals until it's too late. By the time metrics drop, users have already left.

climax.text: The best product teams blend data and empathy — using numbers to detect and conversations to understand.

punchline.text: Data shows the what. Users explain the why.

quiz.question: Why isn't data alone sufficient for product decisions?
options: Data shows what happens but misses emotional signals — qualitative research explains why
Users always know exactly what they want
